stemHtml:
During a lesson in a disorientation trainer with no external vision, a student is told to move the head up and down while the training device rotates about the vertical axis.

Why, during this manoeuvre, does the student suddenly feel dizzy and disoriented?

options.a: The head movements disturb the processing of sensory inputs in the cerebral cortex areas of the student's brain, and subsequently, spatial orientation is lost.
options.b: The head movements disturb the equilibrium between all sensory inputs, and after a certain period of time, the human brain is no longer able to compensate for this.
options.c: Because the training device is rotating, and without outside vision, the interaction between the senses is disturbed when the head is moved.
options.d: The head movement itself has no effect on the disorientation; the rotation of the student about the vertical axis in combination with no outside vision alone account for the disorientation.
